layman_headline: The market is fearful and altcoins look weak — patience is the best trade right now.
layman_explanation: The crypto market is in a 'Fear' phase with a reading of 28 out of 100. Bitcoin is dominating at 56.2%, which means money is flowing away from smaller coins (altcoins) and into Bitcoin for safety. None of the swing trade candidates offer a compelling setup — some are too overbought (DEXE up 101% in a week), others are in confirmed downtrends with bearish chart patterns (GRASS, ZRO, PENGU), and the rest lack sufficient data or conviction. This is a classic environment where sitting on the sidelines preserves capital.
layman_action: Wait for clearer setups. The fear reading is improving (trending up from 24.6 weekly average to 28), which could set up better entries in coming days. Keep powder dry and watch for oversold bounces with volume confirmation.
expert_reasoning: Systematic review of all 10 candidates reveals no high-conviction swing entry. DEXE (+101% 7d, RSI 77) is wildly overbought and chasing here violates risk management. ZRO (RSI 31) is the closest to an oversold bounce play but price is below all MAs with a -0.7 chart bias and no bullish candlestick reversal pattern — entering before confirmation risks catching a falling knife. GRASS (-21% 7d) shows a bearish engulfing candle contradicting any mean-reversion thesis. HTX has the best chart bias (+1.1) but is a micro-cap with negligible volume and RSI already at 58. ETHFI has a decent chart (+0.6) but resistance is only 2.5% away with poor R/R of 0.11x. BTC dominance at 56.2% actively suppresses alt performance. The improving Fear & Greed trend (24.6→28) suggests we may get better setups in 2-5 days as sentiment recovers further.
risk_factors: BTC dominance at 56.2% is actively crushing altcoin performance — any alt entry faces structural headwind
Iran strikes creating geopolitical uncertainty could trigger another leg down across risk assets
Stablecoin market cap shrinking by $10B since May signals capital leaving the crypto ecosystem
watch_list: ZRO: watch for RSI to drop below 30 with a bullish reversal candle (hammer/morning star) at $0.90 support — that would create a high-quality oversold bounce setup
Fear & Greed index: if it crosses above 30-35 in the next 48 hours, altcoin entries become more favorable
BTC price action around $63,000-64,000: a decisive move above $65K could rotate capital back into alts
market_context: The market is in a Fear phase (28/100) with BTC flat and dominance elevated at 56.2%, creating a hostile environment for altcoin swing trades. Geopolitical tensions (Iran strikes) and shrinking stablecoin supply add macro headwinds, though the improving fear trend suggests conditions may improve soon.
context_banner: No swing candidates pass the quality filter — elevated BTC dominance and fearful sentiment demand patience over action.

key_signals.description: Fear at 28/100 with improving trend (up from 24.6 weekly avg) — conditions are getting better but not yet favorable enough for alt entries.
ZRO RSI at 31 is the most oversold candidate but lacks bullish reversal confirmation on the chart, making it premature to enter.
Stablecoin market cap shrinking by $10B since May signals capital outflows from crypto, reducing fuel for new rallies.
Bitcoin nearing Fidelity's power law support line tracked since 2015 — a bounce here could eventually benefit alts, but hasn't triggered yet.
Robinhood L2 sparking ETH optimism and ETH derivatives reset noted by Blockworks could create future opportunities in ETH-ecosystem plays like ETHFI once technicals confirm.
